cards.title: E cell and spontaneity
cards.questionImage: Reference/board-01 e-cell-and-spontaneity-parent E cell and spontaneity [Q].png
cards.answerImage: Reference/board-01 e-cell-and-spontaneity-parent E cell and spontaneity [ans].png

cards.extensionFile: extension question/board-01 e-cell-and-spontaneity-parent E cell and spontaneity.md

extensionQuestions.question: What equation links ΔG° and E°cell?
What does a positive E°cell indicate about spontaneity?
extensionQuestions.answer: ΔG° is negative, so the reaction is spontaneous.

extensionQuestions.question: What does a more positive E° value indicate?
extensionQuestions.answer: The forward reduction reaction is more favoured.

cards.title: E cell and spontaneity

cards.answerImage: Reference/board-01 e-cell-and-spontaneity E cell and spontaneity [ans].png

cards.extensionFile: extension question/board-01 e-cell-and-spontaneity E cell and spontaneity.md

extensionQuestions.question: What equation links ΔG° and E°cell?
What does a negative E°cell indicate about spontaneity?
extensionQuestions.answer: ΔG° is positive, so the reaction is not spontaneous.

cards.title: Electrochemical cell setup
cards.extensionFile: extension question/board-01 electrochemical-cell-setup Electrochemical cell setup.md

extensionQuestions.question: What are the two roles of the salt bridge in an electrochemical cell?
extensionQuestions.answer: It ensures charge neutrality and completes the circuit.

extensionQuestions.question: What are the standard conditions shown for the H⁺/H₂ half-cell?
extensionQuestions.answer: H₂(g) at 1 bar, H⁺(aq) at 1 mol dm⁻³, and 298 K.

extensionQuestions.question: What is needed for a metal/metal ion half-cell?
extensionQuestions.answer: A metal electrode dipped into a solution containing its metal ions.

extensionQuestions.question: Why is an inert Pt electrode used in gas/ion and ion/ion half-cells?
extensionQuestions.answer: It provides a conducting surface when there is no solid metal electrode in the redox pair.

extensionQuestions.question: What happens to E°(Al³⁺/Al) when [Al³⁺] increases?
extensionQuestions.answer: The equilibrium shifts right, so the electrode potential becomes less negative/ more positive
